core_mechanism: large-N extreme-value / order-statistics asymptotics of an ensemble of N independent stochastic processes, in which the characteristic scale grows logarithmically in N and is corrected by a slowly-converging asymptotic expansion in inverse powers of ln(N) with iterated (double-)logarithmic terms
brief_reason: The three distinct problems (distinct sites explored, first-passage/exit times of the first j of N walkers, and trapping times) all concern N>>1 independent walkers and their extremes/order statistics, and share the same solution form as a series in ln^{-n}(N) ln^m ln(N), the signature of extreme-value asymptotics in the number of independent samples.
